BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\ninterface ICCTPTokenMessenger {\n    function depositForBurn(\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint32 destinationDomain,\n        bytes32 mintRecipient,\n        address burnToken,\n        bytes32 destinationCaller,\n        uint256 maxFee,\n        uint32 minFinalityThreshold\n    ) external;\n\n    function depositForBurnWithHook(\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint32 destinationDomain,\n        bytes32 mintRecipient,\n        address burnToken,\n        bytes32 destinationCaller,\n        uint256 maxFee,\n        uint32 minFinalityThreshold,\n        bytes memory hookData\n    ) external;\n\n    function getMinFeeAmount(uint256 amount) external view returns (uint256);\n}\n\ninterface ICCTPMessageTransmitter {\n    function sendMessage(\n        uint32 destinationDomain,\n        bytes32 recipient,\n        bytes32 destinationCaller,\n        uint32 minFinalityThreshold,\n        bytes memory messageBody\n    ) external;\n\n    function receiveMessage(bytes calldata message, bytes calldata attestation)\n        external\n        returns (bool);\n}\n\ninterface IMessageHandlerV2 {\n    /**\n     * @notice Handles an incoming finalized message from an IReceiverV2\n     * @dev Finalized messages have finality threshold values greater than or equal to 2000\n     * @param sourceDomain The source domain of the message\n     * @param sender The sender of the message\n     * @param finalityThresholdExecuted the finality threshold at which the message was attested to\n     * @param messageBody The raw bytes of the message body\n     * @return success True, if successful; false, if not.\n     */\n    function handleReceiveFinalizedMessage(\n        uint32 sourceDomain,\n        bytes32 sender,\n        uint32 finalityThresholdExecuted,\n        bytes calldata messageBody\n    ) external returns (bool);\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Handles an incoming unfinalized message from an IReceiverV2\n     * @dev Unfinalized messages have finality threshold values less than 2000\n     * @param sourceDomain The source domain of the message\n     * @param sender The sender of the message\n     * @param finalityThresholdExecuted The finality threshold at which the message was attested to\n     * @param messageBody The raw bytes of the message body\n     * @return success True, if successful; false, if not.\n     */\n    function handleReceiveUnfinalizedMessage(\n        uint32 sourceDomain,\n        bytes32 sender,\n        uint32 finalityThresholdExecuted,\n        bytes calldata messageBody\n    ) external returns (bool);\n}\n","keccak256":"0x67c60863105b4a694bc6edc77777560120262fd4f883a5bbece53f0c70d88e7b","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/interfaces/crosschainV3/IBridgeAdapter.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\n/**\n * @title IBridgeAdapter\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Bridge-agnostic adapter interface used by Master / Remote strategies. The adapter\n *         encapsulates one bridge transport (CCIP, CCTP, OP Stack canonical bridge) so the\n *         strategy stays bridge-ignorant. The adapter owns the envelope shape; the strategy\n *         only ever passes its own opaque `payload` bytes.\n *\n *         A single adapter deployment serves all authorised strategies on its chain, with\n *         per-sender lane configuration held inside the adapter. Each adapter is bound to\n *         one peer chain through the lane configuration.\n */\ninterface IBridgeAdapter {\n    /**\n     * @notice Send a message-only payload to the configured peer.\n     * @dev    Strategy passes opaque `payload`; the adapter wraps `(msg.sender, payload)` into\n     *         its transport envelope. For bridges that require a native fee, caller must\n     *         supply `msg.value >= quoteFee.fee` (the adapter checks). No refund of excess \u2014\n     *         overpayment stays on the adapter and can be recovered by governor via\n     *         `transferToken(address(0), ...)`.\n     */\n    function sendMessage(bytes calldata payload) external payable;\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Send a token transfer alongside a message to the configured peer.\n     * @dev    Adapter pulls `amount` of `token` from `msg.sender` via `safeTransferFrom`,\n     *         then forwards via its bridge transport together with the wrapped envelope.\n     *         Same `msg.value` semantics as `sendMessage`.\n     */\n    function sendMessageAndTokens(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    ) external payable;\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Quote the fee for the operation described by `(token, amount, payload)`.\n     *\n     * @return fee The fee amount, denominated in `feeToken`. When `feeToken == address(0)`\n     *             this is a native (ETH) fee. When non-zero this is an ERC20 fee in that\n     *             token. When the bridge protocol auto-deducts from the bridged amount\n     *             (e.g., CCTP V2 fast-finality), `fee` is informational only \u2014 the actual\n     *             deduction happens transparently inside the bridge, NOT on the caller.\n     * @return feeToken The token the fee is denominated in. `address(0)` means native.\n     * @return requiresExternalPayment True if the caller must supply `fee` of `feeToken`\n     *             alongside the send call (e.g., via `msg.value` for native). False if the\n     *             bridge protocol handles the fee transparently (e.g., deducts from the\n     *             bridged token amount). The strategy reads this flag to decide whether to\n     *             enforce a `msg.value` check; if false, the caller can ignore `fee`\n     *             entirely.\n     *\n     *         The three-value return separates two orthogonal concerns:\n     *           1. Pre-send caller action (do I need to pay anything separately?)\n     *           2. Post-send accounting (the actual deduction is surfaced via\n     *              `IBridgeReceiver.receiveMessage(... uint256 feePaid)` on the receiving\n     *              side, independent of this quote).\n     */\n    function quoteFee(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    )\n        external\n        view\n        returns (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        );\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Per-tx maximum token amount this adapter accepts on outbound, and the implied\n     *         maximum it can deliver on inbound (since outbound and inbound are configured\n     *         as mirror sides of the same protocol lane).\n     *\n     *         Strategies use this in two places:\n     *           - Master.depositAll caps the locally-staged balance by\n     *             `outboundAdapter.maxTransferAmount()` before sending.\n     *           - Master.withdrawAll caps the requested amount by\n     *             `inboundAdapter.maxTransferAmount()`, because Master can't query Remote's\n     *             outbound across chains \u2014 the symmetric inbound adapter holds the same\n     *             protocol-level cap.\n     *\n     *         `0` means \"no enforcement\" (unlimited). Concrete adapters layer additional\n     *         protocol-level constants on top (e.g., CCTPAdapter enforces a hard 10M USDC\n     *         cap regardless of the configured value).\n     */\n    function maxTransferAmount() external view returns (uint256);\n}\n","keccak256":"0xc212efb34e7ad260919d44f2fd6c45507b96a378026e2c29c69e1df5d8423a03","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/interfaces/crosschainV3/IBridgeReceiver.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\n/**\n * @title IBridgeReceiver\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Receiver hook implemented by Master and Remote strategies. The configured inbound\n *         adapter forwards incoming bridge deliveries through this single entry point.\n *\n *         The adapter MUST have transferred any inbound tokens to the strategy before\n *         invoking this function. The `amountReceived` argument carries the actual landed\n *         amount (post any transport-side fee deduction); the strategy accounts on\n *         `amountReceived` and may use `feePaid` for telemetry or sanity checks against the\n *         sender's intended amount carried inside `payload`.\n */\ninterface IBridgeReceiver {\n    /**\n     * @notice Called by the authorised inbound adapter when a message lands.\n     * @param sender         Strategy address on the source chain \u2014 under CREATE3 parity, the\n     *                       same address as the destination strategy on this chain.\n     * @param token          Token delivered alongside the message; `address(0)` for\n     *                       message-only deliveries.\n     * @param amountReceived Actual amount of `token` transferred to this strategy by the\n     *                       adapter immediately before this call (already received).\n     * @param feePaid        Transport-side fee deducted from the sender's intended amount\n     *                       (e.g., CCTP V2 fast-finality fee). Informational; the strategy's\n     *                       accounting is on `amountReceived`.\n     * @param payload        Strategy-owned opaque bytes from the source envelope.\n     */\n    function receiveMessage(\n        address sender,\n        address token,\n        uint256 amountReceived,\n        uint256 feePaid,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    ) external;\n}\n","keccak256":"0x9a33146f1323765e9bf73470a5e1bf3e7e3c34a08f9e360cdd43f8c140cf1979","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/strategies/crosschainV3/adapters/AbstractAdapter.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nimport { IERC20 } from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/IERC20.sol\";\nimport { SafeERC20 } from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/utils/SafeERC20.sol\";\n\nimport { Governable } from \"../../../governance/Governable.sol\";\nimport { IBridgeAdapter } from \"../../../interfaces/crosschainV3/IBridgeAdapter.sol\";\nimport { IBridgeReceiver } from \"../../../interfaces/crosschainV3/IBridgeReceiver.sol\";\n\n/**\n * @title AbstractAdapter\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Shared base for OUSD V3 bridge adapters. One adapter deployment serves a single\n *         (chain, bridge protocol) \u2014 multi-tenant across strategies on that chain, with per-\n *         sender lane configuration. Under CREATE3 cross-chain parity, the peer adapter on\n *         the destination chain shares this contract's own address, so outbound routing and\n *         inbound trust checks both reference `address(this)`.\n *\n *         The base provides:\n *           - `authorised` whitelist gating both outbound (`msg.sender`) and inbound\n *             (`envelopeSender`); a single authorise call wires both directions.\n *           - `laneConfig[sender]` with destination chain selector, paused flag, and\n *             destination-side gas hint. Concrete adapters extend with their own per-lane\n *             extras as separate mappings.\n *           - Strategists list \u2014 accounts that can pause/unpause lanes for fast incident\n *             response. Governor also has these powers.\n *           - Outbound `sendMessage` / `sendMessageAndTokens` that wrap\n *             `(msg.sender, payload)` into a transport envelope, require\n *             `msg.value >= quote`, and refund the excess to the caller.\n *           - Inbound helpers `_validateInbound` (transport identity already verified by\n *             the concrete adapter) and `_deliver` (atomic delivery to the destination\n *             strategy).\n *           - A `transferToken` sweep for stuck tokens / native (governor only).\n *\n *         Concrete adapters implement three internal hooks for the bridge-specific transport\n *         calls: `_sendMessage`, `_sendMessageAndTokens`, `_quoteFee`.\n */\nabstract contract AbstractAdapter is IBridgeAdapter, Governable {\n    using SafeERC20 for IERC20;\n\n    /// @notice Per-lane routing config. One row per authorised sender.\n    struct ChainConfig {\n        bool paused;\n        uint64 chainSelector; // destination chain identifier (protocol-specific encoding)\n        uint32 destGasLimit; // gas hint forwarded to the receive callback on the peer\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Sender \u2192 authorised flag. Gates both outbound `msg.sender` and inbound\n    ///         envelopeSender. CREATE3 parity means the same address represents the same\n    ///         strategy on every chain it lives on.\n    mapping(address => bool) public authorised;\n\n    /// @notice Sender \u2192 lane config. Mutating this changes which destination chain the\n    ///         sender can send to / be received from; treat as governance-grade.\n    mapping(address => ChainConfig) public laneConfig;\n\n    /// @notice Strategists list \u2014 actors permitted to flip the `paused` flag on a lane.\n    ///         The governor also has these powers.\n    mapping(address => bool) public strategists;\n\n    /// @notice Per-tx maximum token amount this adapter accepts on outbound. Governor-set\n    ///         to match the bridge protocol's per-tx limit (CCIP token-lane rate, CCTP V2\n    ///         per-burn cap, etc.). Strategies on the peer chain treat the same value as\n    ///         \"max this adapter can deliver inbound per tx\" to size their withdrawAll-style\n    ///         requests. `0` = no enforcement at this layer (concrete adapters may still\n    ///         apply hard protocol-level constants on top).\n    uint256 public maxTransferAmount;\n\n    event Authorised(address indexed sender, ChainConfig cfg);\n    event Revoked(address indexed sender);\n    event LaneConfigUpdated(address indexed sender, ChainConfig cfg);\n    event LanePaused(address indexed sender);\n    event LaneUnpaused(address indexed sender);\n    event StrategistAdded(address indexed who);\n    event StrategistRemoved(address indexed who);\n    event MaxTransferAmountUpdated(uint256 oldAmount, uint256 newAmount);\n    event MessageSent(\n        address indexed sender,\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint256 feeCharged\n    );\n    event MessageDelivered(\n        address indexed target,\n        address token,\n        uint256 amountReceived,\n        uint256 feePaid\n    );\n\n    /// @dev Reserved for future expansion of this abstract layer (proxy upgradeable).\n    uint256[44] private __gap;\n\n    constructor() {\n        // For standalone deployments (tests, scratch). When behind a proxy, the proxy's\n        // own constructor + initialize ritual is the source of truth \u2014 this assignment is\n        // overwritten as soon as the proxy delegates governance through `_changeGovernor`.\n        _setGovernor(msg.sender);\n    }\n\n    // --- Modifiers ---------------------------------------------------------\n\n    modifier onlyAuthorised() {\n        require(authorised[msg.sender], \"Adapter: not authorised\");\n        _;\n    }\n\n    modifier onlyStrategistOrGovernor() {\n        require(\n            strategists[msg.sender] || isGovernor(),\n onlyGovernor\n    {\n        require(sender != address(0), \"Adapter: zero sender\");\n        // chainSelector may be 0 \u2014 CCTP V2 domain for Ethereum/Sepolia is literally 0.\n        // token amount ceiling. Set to match the bridge\n    ///         protocol's actual per-tx limit (CCIP lane rate, CCTP burn cap, etc.).\n    ///         `0` disables the check (e.g., canonical bridges with no per-tx limit).\n    function setMaxTransferAmount(uint256 _amount) external onlyGovernor {\n        emit MaxTransferAmountUpdated(maxTransferAmount, _amount);\n        maxTransferAmount = _amount;\n    }\n\n    function pauseLane(address sender) external onlyStrategistOrGovernor {\n        require(authorised[sender], \"Adapter: sender not authorised\");\n        laneConfig[sender].paused = true;\n        emit LanePaused(sender);\n    }\n\n    function unpauseLane(address sender) external onlyStrategistOrGovernor {\n        require(authorised[sender], \"Adapter: sender not authorised\");\n        laneConfig[sender].paused = false;\n        emit LaneUnpaused(sender);\n    }\n\n    // --- Governance: recovery ----------------------------------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Sweep a stuck asset (or native via `_asset == address(0)`) to the governor.\n     *         Recovery only \u2014 used to rescue mistaken sends or drain stale refund balances.\n     */\n    function transferToken(address _asset, uint256 _amount)\n        external\n        onlyGovernor\n    {\n        if (_asset == address(0)) {\n            // slither-disable-next-line low-level-calls\n            (bool ok, ) = governor().call{ value: _amount }(\"\");\n            require(ok, \"Adapter: native transfer failed\");\n        } else {\n            IERC20(_asset).safeTransfer(governor(), _amount);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // --- Outbound (IBridgeAdapter) -----------------------------------------\n\n    /// @inheritdoc IBridgeAdapter\n    ///\n    /// @dev No refund on excess. Pool-donation semantics are simpler than per-call refund logic.\n    function sendMessage(bytes calldata payload)\n        external\n        payable\n        override\n        onlyAuthorised\n    {\n        ChainConfig memory cfg = laneConfig[msg.sender];\n        require(!cfg.paused, \"Adapter: lane paused\");\n        bytes memory envelope = _wrap(msg.sender, 0, payload);\n        (uint256 fee, , bool requiresExternalPayment) = _quoteFee(\n            envelope,\n            cfg,\n            address(0),\n            0\n        );\n        // requiresExternalPayment == false means the bridge handles its own fee internally\n        // (e.g., CCTP V2 auto-deducts from the burn amount); msg.value is not consumed.\n        if (requiresExternalPayment) {\n            require(msg.value >= fee, \"Adapter: insufficient fee\");\n        }\n        _sendMessage(envelope, cfg, requiresExternalPayment ? fee : 0);\n        emit MessageSent(msg.sender, address(0), 0, fee);\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc IBridgeAdapter\n    function sendMessageAndTokens(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    ) external payable override onlyAuthorised {\n        require(token != address(0), \"Adapter: zero token\");\n        require(amount > 0, \"Adapter: zero amount\");\n        // Per-tx amount cap. `0` disables the check (canonical bridges, unconfigured).\n        // Reject cleanly here rather than letting the bridge router revert deep inside\n        // its own validation.\n        require(\n            maxTransferAmount == 0 || amount <= maxTransferAmount,\n            \"Adapter: amount above max\"\n        );\n        ChainConfig memory cfg = laneConfig[msg.sender];\n        require(!cfg.paused, \"Adapter: lane paused\");\n        bytes memory envelope = _wrap(msg.sender, amount, payload);\n        (uint256 fee, , bool requiresExternalPayment) = _quoteFee(\n            envelope,\n            cfg,\n            token,\n            amount\n        );\n        if (requiresExternalPayment) {\n            require(msg.value >= fee, \"Adapter: insufficient fee\");\n        }\n        IERC20(token).safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), amount);\n        _sendMessageAndTokens(\n            token,\n            amount,\n            envelope,\n            cfg,\n            requiresExternalPayment ? fee : 0\n        );\n        emit MessageSent(msg.sender, token, amount, fee);\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc IBridgeAdapter\n    function quoteFee(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    )\n        external\n        view\n        override\n        returns (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        )\n    {\n        ChainConfig memory cfg = laneConfig[msg.sender];\n        bytes memory envelope = _wrap(msg.sender, amount, payload);\n        return _quoteFee(envelope, cfg, token, amount);\n    }\n\n    // --- Outbound hooks (concrete adapters implement) ----------------------\n\n    /// @dev Send a message-only envelope through the bridge transport. `fee` is the native\n    ///      value to attach to the underlying bridge call; 0 when the protocol auto-deducts.\n    function _sendMessage(\n        bytes memory envelope,\n        ChainConfig memory cfg,\n        uint256 fee\n    ) internal virtual;\n\n    /// @dev Send a message + tokens through the bridge transport. Same `fee` semantics as\n    ///      `_sendMessage`.\n    function _sendMessageAndTokens(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes memory envelope,\n        ChainConfig memory cfg,\n        uint256 fee\n    ) internal virtual;\n\n    /// @dev Compute the fee details for the outbound op. See `IBridgeAdapter.quoteFee` for\n    ///      the meaning of each return value. The three-value form lets the strategy\n    ///      separate \"is action required?\" from \"what token / how much?\" \u2014 important for\n    ///      bridges like CCTP V2 where the fee is real but auto-deducted (caller takes no\n    ///      action) vs CCIP where the caller must supply native.\n    function _quoteFee(\n        bytes memory envelope,\n        ChainConfig memory cfg,\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount\n    )\n        internal\n        view\n        virtual\n        returns (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        );\n\n    // --- Inbound helpers (concrete adapter calls from its transport entry) --\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Validate an inbound envelope against the configured lane. Concrete adapters\n     *      pass:\n     *        - `srcChain`        \u2014 source chain ID extracted from the bridge transport.\n     *        - `transportSender` \u2014 source-chain caller that originated the bridge tx. Under\n     *                              CREATE3 parity, this must equal `address(this)` (the peer\n     *                              adapter has the same address).\n     *        - `envelope`        \u2014 full wrapped bytes received from the transport.\n     *      Returns the decoded `envelopeSender` (also the destination strategy address on\n     *      this chain), `intendedAmount` (sender's intent for the token leg; 0 for\n     *      message-only), and the strategy-owned `payload`.\n     */\n    function _validateInbound(\n        uint64 srcChain,\n        address transportSender,\n        bytes memory envelope\n    )\n        internal\n        view\n        returns (\n            address envelopeSender,\n            uint256 intendedAmount,\n            bytes memory payload\n        )\n    {\n        (envelopeSender, intendedAmount, payload) = _unwrap(envelope);\n        require(authorised[envelopeSender], \"Adapter: not authorised\");\n        ChainConfig memory cfg = laneConfig[envelopeSender];\n        require(!cfg.paused, \"Adapter: lane paused\");\n        require(srcChain == cfg.chainSelector, \"Adapter: wrong source chain\");\n        require(\n            transportSender == address(this),\n            \"Adapter: not from peer adapter\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Atomically transfer `amountReceived` of `token` to the target strategy and call\n     *      `receiveMessage`. The target strategy address equals `envelopeSender` under\n     *      CREATE3 parity.\n     */\n    function _deliver(\n        address envelopeSender,\n        address token,\n        uint256 amountReceived,\n        uint256 feePaid,\n        bytes memory payload\n    ) internal {\n        if (amountReceived > 0 && token != address(0)) {\n            IERC20(token).safeTransfer(envelopeSender, amountReceived);\n        }\n        IBridgeReceiver(envelopeSender).receiveMessage(\n            envelopeSender,\n            token,\n            amountReceived,\n            feePaid,\n            payload\n        );\n        emit MessageDelivered(envelopeSender, token, amountReceived, feePaid);\n    }\n\n    // --- Envelope wrap / unwrap --------------------------------------------\n\n    /// @dev Header byte length: 20 (sender) + 32 (intendedAmount).\n    uint256 internal constant HEADER_LENGTH = 52;\n\n    /// @dev Wire envelope: 20-byte `sender` + 32-byte `intendedAmount` + opaque `payload`.\n    ///      `intendedAmount` is the token leg the sender intends to land on the destination\n    ///      (0 for message-only). The receiving adapter compares against the actual landed\n    ///      amount to surface any transport-side fee delta to the strategy.\n    function _wrap(\n        address sender,\n        uint256 intendedAmount,\n        bytes memory payload\n    ) internal pure returns (bytes memory) {\n        return abi.encodePacked(sender, intendedAmount, payload);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Inverse of `_wrap`. Reverts when the envelope is shorter than the header.\n    function _unwrap(bytes memory envelope)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (\n            address sender,\n            uint256 intendedAmount,\n            bytes memory payload\n        )\n    {\n        require(envelope.length >= HEADER_LENGTH, \"Adapter: bad envelope\");\n        // Load first 20 bytes as address.\n        // solhint-disable-next-line no-inline-assembly\n        assembly {\n            sender := shr(96, mload(add(envelope, 32)))\n            // intendedAmount lives at offset 20; mload reads 32 bytes from there.\n            intendedAmount := mload(add(envelope, 52))\n        }\n        // Copy the remainder into a new bytes buffer.\n        uint256 payloadLength = envelope.length - HEADER_LENGTH;\n        payload = new bytes(payloadLength);\n        // solhint-disable-next-line no-inline-assembly\n        assembly {\n            let src := add(envelope, 84) // 32-byte length + 20 sender + 32 amount\n            let dst := add(payload, 32)\n            for {\n                let i := 0\n            } lt(i, payloadLength) {\n                i := add(i, 32)\n            } {\n                mstore(add(dst, i), mload(add(src, i)))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // --- Native receive ----------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// @dev Accepts native ETH (e.g., refunds from underlying transports). Concrete adapters\n    ///      may override to add behaviour (e.g., SuperbridgeAdapter wrapping incoming bridge\n    ///      ETH to WETH on the L2 side).\n    receive() external payable virtual {}\n}\n","keccak256":"0xbfedc040932aea63087fc2f87aa27ec468d0ec5b042a8bbaa5cea7078d805037","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/strategies/crosschainV3/adapters/CCTPAdapter.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nimport { IERC20 } from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/IERC20.sol\";\nimport { SafeERC20 } from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/utils/SafeERC20.sol\";\n\nimport { ICCTPTokenMessenger, ICCTPMessageTransmitter, IMessageHandlerV2 } from \"../../../interfaces/cctp/ICCTP.sol\";\nimport { AbstractAdapter } from \"./AbstractAdapter.sol\";\nimport { CCTPMessageHelper } from \"../libraries/CCTPMessageHelper.sol\";\n\n/**\n * @title CCTPAdapter\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Atomic bidirectional adapter over Circle CCTP V2.\n *           - Outbound (`sendMessageAndTokens`): burn USDC via `depositForBurnWithHook` with\n *             the wrapped envelope as the hook data. The recipient mint amount equals the\n *             burn amount minus CCTP's protocol fee (0 for finalised threshold, > 0 for\n *             fast finality). The fee is absorbed by the protocol; the receiving strategy\n *             accounts on the actual landed amount.\n *           - Outbound (`sendMessage`): post a hook-only message via the MessageTransmitter.\n *           - Inbound (`handleReceiveFinalizedMessage`): CCTP MessageTransmitter has minted\n *             USDC to this adapter. Validate source domain + sender against the lane config,\n *             then forward the actual minted amount to the destination strategy.\n *\n *         CCTP has no native bridge fee \u2014 `_quoteFee` returns 0 and the AbstractAdapter's\n *         `msg.value` plumbing simply refunds whatever the caller forwarded.\n */\ncontract CCTPAdapter is AbstractAdapter, IMessageHandlerV2 {\n    using SafeERC20 for IERC20;\n\n    /// @notice CCTP V2 protocol cap per burn. Hard-coded as a `constant` so it can't be\n    ///         raised by governance \u2014 Circle decides this number, not us. Higher values\n    ///         would revert at the TokenMessenger anyway; we reject early for a cleaner\n    ///         error message. If Circle ever raises the cap, this constant gets bumped via\n    ///         contract upgrade, not via governance setter.\n    uint256 public constant MAX_TRANSFER_AMOUNT = 10_000_000 * 10**6; // 10M USDC\n\n    /// @notice USDC on this chain.\n    address public immutable usdcToken;\n    /// @notice Circle CCTP V2 Token Messenger.\n    ICCTPTokenMessenger public immutable tokenMessenger;\n    /// @notice Circle CCTP V2 Message Transmitter (message-only sends + inbound delivery).\n    ICCTPMessageTransmitter public immutable messageTransmitter;\n\n    /// @notice Minimum finality threshold sent on every transfer. Range: 1000\u20132000.\n    ///         2000 = finalised (zero protocol fee, ~13 minute delay on Ethereum).\n    ///         1000\u20131999 = fast finality (non-zero token-side fee, sub-minute delivery).\n    ///\n    ///         NOT initialised at declaration \u2014 that would only set the storage slot on\n    ///         the implementation, not on the proxy. Governor must call\n    ///         `setMinFinalityThreshold` post-deploy. Send-side guard catches the\n    ///         pre-init mistake with a clear revert message.\n    uint32 public minFinalityThreshold;\n\n    /// @notice Lower bound on USDC transfers; governor-settable. Avoids dust burns that\n    ///         waste gas + CCTP attestation latency on negligible amounts.\n    uint256 public minTransferAmount;\n\n    /// @notice Account allowed to invoke `relay(message, attestation)` \u2014 the off-chain\n    ///         attestation poller / relayer. Single address; governor-settable. CCTP is\n    ///         pull-driven so this role is required at the adapter level; CCIP and\n    ///         Superbridge don't need it.\n    address public operator;\n\n    event MinFinalityThresholdUpdated(uint32 oldThreshold, uint32 newThreshold);\n    event MinTransferAmountUpdated(uint256 oldAmount, uint256 newAmount);\n    event OperatorUpdated(address oldOperator, address newOperator);\n    event MessageRelayed(address indexed by, uint32 sourceDomain);\n\n    constructor(\n        address _usdcToken,\n        ICCTPTokenMessenger _tokenMessenger,\n        ICCTPMessageTransmitter _messageTransmitter\n    ) {\n        require(_usdcToken != address(0), \"CCTP: zero usdc\");\n        require(address(_tokenMessenger) != address(0), \"CCTP: zero messenger\");\n        require(\n            address(_messageTransmitter) != address(0),\n            \"CCTP: zero transmitter\"\n        );\n        usdcToken = _usdcToken;\n        tokenMessenger = _tokenMessenger;\n        messageTransmitter = _messageTransmitter;\n    }\n\n    modifier onlyCCTP() {\n        require(\n            msg.sender == address(messageTransmitter),\n            \"CCTP: not message transmitter\"\n        );\n        _;\n    }\n\n    modifier onlyOperator() {\n        require(msg.sender == operator, \"CCTP: not operator\");\n        _;\n    }\n\n    function setMinFinalityThreshold(uint32 _t) external onlyGovernor {\n        require(_t >= 1000 && _t <= 2000, \"CCTP: bad threshold\");\n        emit MinFinalityThresholdUpdated(minFinalityThreshold, _t);\n        minFinalityThreshold = _t;\n    }\n\n    function setMinTransferAmount(uint256 _amount) external onlyGovernor {\n        emit MinTransferAmountUpdated(minTransferAmount, _amount);\n        minTransferAmount = _amount;\n    }\n\n    function setOperator(address _operator) external onlyGovernor {\n        emit OperatorUpdated(operator, _operator);\n        operator = _operator;\n    }\n\n    // --- Relay (operator-driven inbound finalisation) ---------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Operator entry point: hand a Circle-signed CCTP message + attestation pair\n     *         to the local MessageTransmitter, then dispatch the payload to the destination\n     *         strategy.\n     *\n     *         CCTP V2 has two on-wire message shapes that both arrive here:\n     *\n     *         1. **Burn-message + hook** (sourced from `TokenMessenger.depositForBurnWithHook`).\n     *            The transport `sender` is the source-side `TokenMessenger`. The transport\n     *            `recipient` is the destination `TokenMessenger`, NOT this adapter. The\n     *            body is a CCTP burn body containing burnToken / mintRecipient / amount /\n     *            msgSender / feeExecuted / hookData. Auto-dispatch via\n     *            `handleReceiveMessage` on the mintRecipient is V2.1-only and not\n     *            universally available across Circle's chain deployments, so we DON'T rely\n     *            on it. Instead: we call `messageTransmitter.receiveMessage` (which credits\n     *            USDC to this adapter as the configured mintRecipient), then parse the burn\n     *            body ourselves and call `_deliver` with the authoritative `amount -\n     *            feeExecuted` and the hookData (our application envelope). This mirrors\n     *            the older `AbstractCCTPIntegrator.relay()` pattern, which has been\n     *            exercised in production.\n     *\n     *         2. **Pure message** (sourced from `MessageTransmitter.sendMessage`). Transport\n     *            `sender` and `recipient` are both this adapter (CREATE3 parity). The body\n     *            is our application envelope directly. `messageTransmitter.receiveMessage`\n     *            triggers our own `handleReceiveFinalizedMessage` hook, which calls\n     *            `_handleInbound` and dispatches.\n     */\n    function relay(bytes calldata message, bytes calldata attestation)\n        external\n        onlyOperator\n    {\n        (\n            uint32 version,\n            uint32 sourceDomain,\n            address transportSender,\n            address transportRecipient,\n            bytes memory body\n        ) = CCTPMessageHelper.decodeMessageHeader(message);\n        require(\n            version == CCTPMessageHelper.CCTP_V2_VERSION,\n            \"CCTP: bad msg version\"\n        );\n\n        // Burn messages have the source TokenMessenger as their transport sender. Pure\n        // messages have this adapter as both transport sender and recipient (CREATE3\n        // parity).\n        if (transportSender == address(tokenMessenger)) {\n            _relayBurn(sourceDomain, body, message, attestation);\n        } else {\n            require(transportRecipient == address(this), \"CCTP: not for us\");\n            require(\n                messageTransmitter.receiveMessage(message, attestation),\n                \"CCTP: relay failed\"\n            );\n            // MessageTransmitter has now invoked our `handleReceiveFinalizedMessage` (or\n            // unfinalized variant). Nothing more to do here.\n        }\n        emit MessageRelayed(msg.sender, sourceDomain);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Burn-message path. Parse the burn body for authoritative amount/fee/hookData,\n    ///      then `receiveMessage` to credit USDC, then validate + dispatch.\n    function _relayBurn(\n        uint32 sourceDomain,\n        bytes memory body,\n        bytes calldata message,\n        bytes calldata attestation\n    ) internal {\n        (\n            address burnToken,\n            uint256 amount,\n            address msgSender,\n            uint256 feeExecuted,\n            bytes memory hookData\n        ) = CCTPMessageHelper.decodeBurnBody(body);\n        require(burnToken == usdcToken, \"CCTP: bad burn token\");\n\n        uint256 balanceBefore = IERC20(usdcToken).balanceOf(address(this));\n        require(\n            messageTransmitter.receiveMessage(message, attestation),\n            \"CCTP: relay failed\"\n        );\n        uint256 landed = _landedAmount(\n            IERC20(usdcToken).balanceOf(address(this)) - balanceBefore,\n            amount - feeExecuted\n        );\n        _dispatchBurn(\n            sourceDomain,\n            msgSender,\n            hookData,\n            landed,\n            feeExecuted,\n            amount\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Choose the authoritative landed amount: prefer the smaller of the actual\n    ///      mint delta and the expected `amount - feeExecuted`. This isolates donations\n    ///      (extra balance arriving between the snapshot and the mint stays on the\n    ///      adapter) and also defends against short mints (deliver what we actually got).\n    function _landedAmount(uint256 minted, uint256 expected)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        return minted < expected ? minted : expected;\n    }\n\n    function _dispatchBurn(\n        uint32 sourceDomain,\n        address msgSender,\n        bytes memory hookData,\n        uint256 landed,\n        uint256 feeExecuted,\n        uint256 burnAmount\n    ) internal {\n        (\n            address envelopeSender,\n            uint256 intendedAmount,\n            bytes memory payload\n        ) = _validateInbound(uint64(sourceDomain), msgSender, hookData);\n        // Sanity: source-side intent equals the full burn `amount` (the application's\n        // pre-fee intent). `intendedAmount == 0` is permitted for envelopes that don't\n        // pre-set the amount.\n        require(\n            intendedAmount == 0 || intendedAmount == burnAmount,\n            \"CCTP: intent mismatch\"\n        );\n        _deliver(envelopeSender, usdcToken, landed, feeExecuted, payload);\n    }\n\n    // --- Outbound hooks ----------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// @dev CCTP V2 has NO native fee. The protocol fee (when fast finality is used) is\n    ///      deducted by CCTP itself from the burned token amount \u2014 the caller doesn't need\n    ///      to supply anything separately. We report this as `requiresExternalPayment =\n    ///      false` so the strategy skips the msg.value check entirely.\n    ///\n    ///      For token-carrying sends we still report `fee = getMinFeeAmount(amount)` and\n    ///      `feeToken = usdcToken` for telemetry/observability; this is the upper bound\n    ///      the protocol could take. For message-only sends, no token, no fee.\n    function _quoteFee(\n        bytes memory, // envelope\n        ChainConfig memory, // cfg\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount\n    )\n        internal\n        view\n        override\n        returns (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        )\n    {\n        if (token == address(0) || amount == 0) {\n            return (0, address(0), false);\n        }\n        fee = tokenMessenger.getMinFeeAmount(amount);\n        feeToken = usdcToken;\n        requiresExternalPayment = false;\n    }\n\n    function _sendMessage(\n        bytes memory envelope,\n        ChainConfig memory cfg,\n        uint256 /* fee */\n    ) internal override {\n        // Hook-only message via the transmitter (no token leg). destinationCaller is the\n        // peer adapter address (CREATE3 parity) so only it can finalise on the destination.\n        require(minFinalityThreshold > 0, \"CCTP: threshold not set\");\n        messageTransmitter.sendMessage(\n            uint32(cfg.chainSelector),\n            _addressToBytes32(address(this)),\n            _addressToBytes32(address(this)),\n            minFinalityThreshold,\n            envelope\n        );\n    }\n\n    function _sendMessageAndTokens(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes memory envelope,\n        ChainConfig memory cfg,\n        uint256 /* fee */\n    ) internal override {\n        require(token == usdcToken, \"CCTP: token must be usdc\");\n        require(minFinalityThreshold > 0, \"CCTP: threshold not set\");\n        // Bounds: dust floor (governor-set) + Circle's hard per-burn cap. AbstractAdapter\n        // already enforces `maxTransferAmount` if set; we ALSO enforce the protocol-level\n        // constant so an under-configured maxTransferAmount can't accidentally allow a\n        // larger burn than CCTP itself accepts.\n        require(amount >= minTransferAmount, \"CCTP: amount below min\");\n        require(amount <= MAX_TRANSFER_AMOUNT, \"CCTP: amount above CCTP cap\");\n\n        // CCTP V2 will deduct an actual fee (<= maxFee) from the burn; recipient mints the\n        // remainder. We pass maxFee as the upper bound the protocol authorises; with the\n        // default `minFinalityThreshold = 2000` (finalised) the protocol fee is 0.\n        uint256 maxFee = tokenMessenger.getMinFeeAmount(amount);\n        IERC20(token).safeApprove(address(tokenMessenger), amount);\n        tokenMessenger.depositForBurnWithHook(\n            amount,\n            uint32(cfg.chainSelector),\n            _addressToBytes32(address(this)), // mintRecipient = peer adapter\n            token,\n            _addressToBytes32(address(this)), // destinationCaller = peer adapter\n            maxFee,\n            minFinalityThreshold,\n            envelope\n        );\n    }\n\n    function _addressToBytes32(address _addr) internal pure returns (bytes32) {\n        return bytes32(uint256(uint160(_addr)));\n    }\n\n    function _bytes32ToAddress(bytes32 _b) internal pure returns (address) {\n        return address(uint160(uint256(_b)));\n    }\n\n    // --- Inbound (IMessageHandlerV2) ---------------------------------------\n\n    /// @inheritdoc IMessageHandlerV2\n    function handleReceiveFinalizedMessage(\n        uint32 sourceDomain,\n        bytes32 sender,\n        uint32, // finalityThresholdExecuted\n        bytes calldata messageBody\n    ) external override onlyCCTP returns (bool) {\n        _handleInbound(sourceDomain, sender, messageBody);\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc IMessageHandlerV2\n    /// @dev Accepts pre-finalised inbound when CCTP has executed at least the configured\n    ///      `minFinalityThreshold`. This is how fast-finality (1000 <= threshold < 2000)\n    ///      actually delivers \u2014 MessageTransmitter routes via this handler when\n    ///      `finalityThresholdExecuted < 2000`, and we accept if it's >= our floor.\n    ///\n    ///      If `minFinalityThreshold == 2000` (default for finalised-only deployments),\n    ///      this rejects every unfinalised callback \u2014 the strict-finalised mode.\n    ///\n    ///      If `minFinalityThreshold == 0` (governor hasn't called the setter yet), we\n    ///      reject everything, defensive against pre-init relays.\n    function handleReceiveUnfinalizedMessage(\n        uint32 sourceDomain,\n        bytes32 sender,\n        uint32 finalityThresholdExecuted,\n        bytes calldata messageBody\n    ) external override onlyCCTP returns (bool) {\n        require(minFinalityThreshold > 0, \"CCTP: threshold not set\");\n        require(\n            finalityThresholdExecuted >= minFinalityThreshold,\n            \"CCTP: insufficient finality\"\n        );\n        _handleInbound(sourceDomain, sender, messageBody);\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Pure-message-only inbound hook. The MessageTransmitter calls this on us\n    ///      directly only for message-only sends (no token leg). Burn messages flow\n    ///      through `relay()`'s manual parsing path instead \u2014 we don't take the chance\n    ///      that CCTP's auto-callback fires only on V2.1 chains.\n    ///\n    ///      `messageBody` here IS our application envelope (because `sendMessage`\n    ///      forwards it verbatim to the recipient hook). `intendedAmount` should be 0\n    ///      since this is the no-token path; reject otherwise to surface design drift\n    ///      early.\n    function _handleInbound(\n        uint32 sourceDomain,\n        bytes32 sender,\n        bytes calldata messageBody\n    ) internal {\n        (\n            address envelopeSender,\n            uint256 intendedAmount,\n            bytes memory payload\n        ) = _validateInbound(\n                uint64(sourceDomain),\n                _bytes32ToAddress(sender),\n                messageBody\n            );\n        require(intendedAmount == 0, \"CCTP: token leg via pure-message path\");\n        _deliver(envelopeSender, address(0), 0, 0, payload);\n    }\n}\n","keccak256":"0x14993f3e31b10de6c6ad9472f3eee2551ddad558d55d56c539512f28e0290884","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/strategies/crosschainV3/libraries/CCTPMessageHelper.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nimport { BytesHelper } from \"../../../utils/BytesHelper.sol\";\n\n/**\n * @title CCTPMessageHelper\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Minimal decoder for the CCTP V2 transport-level message header. Used by\n *         `CCTPAdapter.relay` to do cheap pre-validation (correct CCTP message version,\n *         correct on-chain recipient) before paying the gas for attestation verification\n *         and the downstream `handleReceiveFinalizedMessage` callback.\n *\n *         The CCTP V2 wire format is owned by Circle and looks like:\n *           [0..4)     uint32  version\n *           [4..8)     uint32  sourceDomain\n *           [8..12)    uint32  destinationDomain\n *           [12..44)   bytes32 nonce\n *           [44..76)   bytes32 sender              (right-aligned address)\n *           [76..108)  bytes32 recipient           (right-aligned address)\n *           [108..140) bytes32 destinationCaller   (right-aligned address)\n *           [140..144) uint32  minFinalityThreshold\n *           [144..148) uint32  finalityThresholdExecuted\n *           [148..]    bytes   messageBody         (our application envelope)\n *\n *         See https://developers.circle.com/cctp/technical-guide#message-header for the\n *         authoritative spec.\n */\nlibrary CCTPMessageHelper {\n    using BytesHelper for bytes;\n\n    /// @notice Wire-format version of CCTP V2 messages.\n    uint32 internal constant CCTP_V2_VERSION = 1;\n\n    uint256 private constant VERSION_INDEX = 0;\n    uint256 private constant SOURCE_DOMAIN_INDEX = 4;\n    uint256 private constant SENDER_INDEX = 44;\n    uint256 private constant RECIPIENT_INDEX = 76;\n    uint256 private constant MESSAGE_BODY_INDEX = 148;\n\n    /// @notice Inner burn-message body offsets for CCTP V2 burn messages. The burn body is\n    ///         what TokenMessenger constructs and ships inside the transport `messageBody`\n    ///         field when `depositForBurnWithHook` is called. We parse it manually in\n    ///         `CCTPAdapter.relay()` so the adapter has the authoritative `amount`,\n    ///         `feeExecuted`, and `hookData` rather than relying on\n    ///         `IERC20.balanceOf(adapter)` (susceptible to donations) or on the\n    ///         `IMessageHandlerV2` callback (which behaves differently across CCTP V2.0\n    ///         and V2.1 deployments).\n    ///\n    ///         Ref: https://github.com/circlefin/evm-cctp-contracts/blob/master/src/messages/v2/BurnMessageV2.sol\n    uint256 private constant BURN_BODY_VERSION_INDEX = 0;\n    uint256 private constant BURN_BODY_BURN_TOKEN_INDEX = 4;\n    uint256 private constant BURN_BODY_MINT_RECIPIENT_INDEX = 36;\n    uint256 private constant BURN_BODY_AMOUNT_INDEX = 68;\n    uint256 private constant BURN_BODY_MESSAGE_SENDER_INDEX = 100;\n    uint256 private constant BURN_BODY_FEE_EXECUTED_INDEX = 164;\n    uint256 private constant BURN_BODY_HOOK_DATA_INDEX = 228;\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Split a CCTP V2 wire message into its transport header fields plus the inner\n     *         `messageBody`. The body is either:\n     *           - a burn-message body (for `depositForBurnWithHook`-sourced messages), or\n     *           - the raw application envelope (for `MessageTransmitter.sendMessage`).\n     * @param message The CCTP V2 wire message bytes as received from Circle's attestation API.\n     */\n    function decodeMessageHeader(bytes memory message)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (\n            uint32 version,\n            uint32 sourceDomain,\n            address sender,\n            address recipient,\n            bytes memory messageBody\n        )\n    {\n        version = message.extractUint32(VERSION_INDEX);\n        sourceDomain = message.extractUint32(SOURCE_DOMAIN_INDEX);\n        sender = message.extractAddress(SENDER_INDEX);\n        recipient = message.extractAddress(RECIPIENT_INDEX);\n        messageBody = message.extractSlice(MESSAGE_BODY_INDEX, message.length);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Decode a CCTP V2 burn-message body into its authoritative fields. Use this\n     *         when the transport header's `sender` indicates the message originated from\n     *         the source-side TokenMessenger (i.e., a `depositForBurnWithHook` rather than\n     *         a plain `sendMessage`).\n     * @param body The inner CCTP V2 burn message body.\n     * @return burnToken The token burned on source (must equal local USDC).\n     * @return amount Source-side burn amount.\n     * @return msgSender The source-side caller of `depositForBurnWithHook` (peer adapter\n     *                  under CREATE3 parity).\n     * @return feeExecuted Protocol fee deducted from `amount` on destination. `amount -\n     *                    feeExecuted` USDC arrives at the mintRecipient.\n     * @return hookData Opaque payload set by the source side via the `hookData` arg of\n     *                  `depositForBurnWithHook`. This is our application envelope.\n     */\n    function decodeBurnBody(bytes memory body)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (\n            address burnToken,\n            uint256 amount,\n            address msgSender,\n            uint256 feeExecuted,\n            bytes memory hookData\n        )\n    {\n        require(\n            body.length >= BURN_BODY_HOOK_DATA_INDEX,\n            \"CCTP: burn body too short\"\n        );\n        burnToken = body.extractAddress(BURN_BODY_BURN_TOKEN_INDEX);\n        amount = body.extractUint256(BURN_BODY_AMOUNT_INDEX);\n        msgSender = body.extractAddress(BURN_BODY_MESSAGE_SENDER_INDEX);\n        feeExecuted = body.extractUint256(BURN_BODY_FEE_EXECUTED_INDEX);\n        hookData = body.extractSlice(BURN_BODY_HOOK_DATA_INDEX, body.length);\n    }\n}\n","keccak256":"0xeba33322ccea6aebf5be23548624de2cd8e85b6b2d53b9a4c9bfc28f81f46b4e","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/utils/BytesHelper.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nuint256 constant UINT32_LENGTH = 4;\nuint256 constant UINT64_LENGTH = 8;\nuint256 constant UINT256_LENGTH = 32;\n// Address is 20 bytes, but we expect the data to be padded with 0s to 32 bytes\nuint256 constant ADDRESS_LENGTH = 32;\n// Raw 20-byte address (no padding), used for abi.encodePacked envelopes.\nuint256 constant ADDRESS_PACKED_LENGTH = 20;\n\nlibrary BytesHelper {\n    /**\n     * @dev Extract a slice from bytes memory\n     * @param data The bytes memory to slice\n     * @param start The start index (inclusive)\n     * @param end The end index (exclusive)\n     * @return result A new bytes memory containing the slice\n     */\n    function extractSlice(\n        bytes memory data,\n        uint256 start,\n        uint256 end\n    ) internal pure returns (bytes memory) {\n        require(end >= start, \"Invalid slice range\");\n        require(end <= data.length, \"Slice end exceeds data length\");\n\n        uint256 length = end - start;\n        bytes memory result = new bytes(length);\n\n        // Simple byte-by-byte copy\n        for (uint256 i = 0; i < length; i++) {\n            result[i] = data[start + i];\n        }\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Decode a uint32 from a bytes memory\n     * @param data The bytes memory to decode\n     * @return uint32 The decoded uint32\n     */\n    function decodeUint32(bytes memory data) internal pure returns (uint32) {\n        require(data.length == 4, \"Invalid data length\");\n        return uint32(uint256(bytes32(data)) >> 224);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Extract a uint32 from a bytes memory\n     * @param data The bytes memory to extract from\n     * @param start The start index (inclusive)\n     * @return uint32 The extracted uint32\n     */\n    function extractUint32(bytes memory data, uint256 start)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint32)\n    {\n        return decodeUint32(extractSlice(data, start, start + UINT32_LENGTH));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Decode a uint64 from a bytes memory\n     * @param data The bytes memory to decode\n     * @return uint64 The decoded uint64\n     */\n    function decodeUint64(bytes memory data) internal pure returns (uint64) {\n        require(data.length == 8, \"Invalid data length\");\n        return uint64(uint256(bytes32(data)) >> 192);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Extract a uint64 from a bytes memory\n     * @param data The bytes memory to extract from\n     * @param start The start index (inclusive)\n     * @return uint64 The extracted uint64\n     */\n    function extractUint64(bytes memory data, uint256 start)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint64)\n    {\n        return decodeUint64(extractSlice(data, start, start + UINT64_LENGTH));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Decode an address from a bytes memory.\n     *      Expects the data to be padded with 0s to 32 bytes.\n     * @param data The bytes memory to decode\n     * @return address The decoded address\n     */\n    function decodeAddress(bytes memory data) internal pure returns (address) {\n        // We expect the data to be padded with 0s, so length is 32 not 20\n        require(data.length == 32, \"Invalid data length\");\n        return abi.decode(data, (address));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Extract an address from a bytes memory\n     * @param data The bytes memory to extract from\n     * @param start The start index (inclusive)\n     * @return address The extracted address\n     */\n    function extractAddress(bytes memory data, uint256 start)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (address)\n    {\n        return decodeAddress(extractSlice(data, start, start + ADDRESS_LENGTH));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Extract a 20-byte (unpadded) address from a bytes memory. Use this when the\n     *      source is `abi.encodePacked(..., address, ...)` rather than `abi.encode(...)`,\n     *      where addresses occupy 20 bytes instead of 32.\n     * @param data The bytes memory to extract from\n     * @param start The start index (inclusive)\n     * @return result The extracted address\n     */\n    function extractAddressPacked(bytes memory data, uint256 start)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (address result)\n    {\n        require(\n            data.length >= start + ADDRESS_PACKED_LENGTH,\n            \"Invalid data length\"\n        );\n        // solhint-disable-next-line no-inline-assembly\n        assembly {\n            // Load 32 bytes starting at the address offset; the address occupies the high\n            // 20 bytes when read from packed encoding, so right-shift by 96 (= 12 * 8) bits.\n            result := shr(96, mload(add(add(data, 0x20), start)))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Decode a uint256 from a bytes memory\n     * @param data The bytes memory to decode\n     * @return uint256 The decoded uint256\n     */\n    function decodeUint256(bytes memory data) internal pure returns (uint256) {\n        require(data.length == 32, \"Invalid data length\");\n        return abi.decode(data, (uint256));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Extract a uint256 from a bytes memory\n     * @param data The bytes memory to extract from\n     * @param start The start index (inclusive)\n     * @return uint256 The extracted uint256\n     */\n    function extractUint256(bytes memory data, uint256 start)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        return decodeUint256(extractSlice(data, start, start + UINT256_LENGTH));\n    }\n}\n","keccak256":"0xa1aada918a16c556117a08463b039f806dd8c3af2f4ca8850b05643084d81dc3","license":"BUSL-1.1"}},"version":1}

handleReceiveFinalizedMessage(uint32,byt.details: Finalized messages have finality threshold values greater than or equal to 2000

params.finalityThresholdExecuted: the finality threshold at which the message was attested to
params.messageBody: The raw bytes of the message body
params.sourceDomain: The source domain of the message

returns._0: success True, if successful; false, if not.

handleReceiveUnfinalizedMessage(uint32,b.details: Accepts pre-finalised inbound when CCTP has executed at least the configured      `minFinalityThreshold`. This is how fast-finality (1000 <= threshold < 2000)      actually delivers — MessageTransmitter routes via this handler when      `finalityThresholdExecuted < 2000`, and we accept if it's >= our floor.      If `minFinalityThreshold == 2000` (default for finalised-only deployments),      this rejects every unfinalised callback — the strict-finalised mode.      If `minFinalityThreshold == 0` (governor hasn't called the setter yet), we      reject everything, defensive against pre-init relays.

params.finalityThresholdExecuted: The finality threshold at which the message was attested to
params.messageBody: The raw bytes of the message body
params.sourceDomain: The source domain of the message

returns._0: success True, if successful; false, if not.

returns.fee: The fee amount, denominated in `feeToken`. When `feeToken == address(0)`             this is a native (ETH) fee. When non-zero this is an ERC20 fee in that             token. When the bridge protocol auto-deducts from the bridged amount             (e.g., CCTP V2 fast-finality), `fee` is informational only — the actual             deduction happens transparently inside the bridge, NOT on the caller.
returns.feeToken: The token the fee is denominated in. `address(0)` means native.
returns.requiresExternalPayment: True if the caller must supply `fee` of `feeToken`             alongside the send call (e.g., via `msg.value` for native). False if the             bridge protocol handles the fee transparently (e.g., deducts from the             bridged token amount). The strategy reads this flag to decide whether to             enforce a `msg.value` check; if false, the caller can ignore `fee`             entirely.         The three-value return separates two orthogonal concerns:           1. Pre-send caller action (do I need to pay anything separately?)           2. Post-send accounting (the actual deduction is surfaced via              `IBridgeReceiver.receiveMessage(... uint256 feePaid)` on the receiving              side, independent of this quote).

sendMessage(bytes).details: No refund on excess. Overpayment stays on the adapter; recover via      `transferToken(address(0), amount)` (governor-only). Rationale: refunds      add code surface, and the strategy quotes fees itself before calling — overpay      should be rare. Pool-donation semantics are simpler than per-call refund logic.

sendMessageAndTokens(address,uint256,byt.details: Adapter pulls `amount` of `token` from `msg.sender` via `safeTransferFrom`,         then forwards via its bridge transport together with the wrapped envelope.         Same `msg.value` semantics as `sendMessage`.

MAX_TRANSFER_AMOUNT().notice: CCTP V2 protocol cap per burn. Hard-coded as a `constant` so it can't be         raised by governance — Circle decides this number, not us. Higher values         would revert at the TokenMessenger anyway; we reject early for a cleaner         error message. If Circle ever raises the cap, this constant gets bumped via         contract upgrade, not via governance setter.

authorise(address,(bool,uint64,uint32)).notice: Authorise `sender` to use this adapter and register its lane config.         Authorisation is bidirectional: the same `sender` is recognised both as         outbound `msg.sender` and as inbound `envelopeSender`.

authorised(address).notice: Sender → authorised flag. Gates both outbound `msg.sender` and inbound         envelopeSender. CREATE3 parity means the same address represents the same         strategy on every chain it lives on.

claimGovernance().notice: Claim Governance of the contract to a new account (`newGovernor`). Can only be called by the new Governor.

governor().notice: Returns the address of the current Governor.

handleReceiveFinalizedMessage(uint32,byt.notice: Handles an incoming finalized message from an IReceiverV2

handleReceiveUnfinalizedMessage(uint32,b.notice: Handles an incoming unfinalized message from an IReceiverV2

isGovernor().notice: Returns true if the caller is the current Governor.

laneConfig(address).notice: Sender → lane config. Mutating this changes which destination chain the         sender can send to / be received from; treat as governance-grade.

maxTransferAmount().notice: Per-tx maximum token amount this adapter accepts on outbound. Governor-set         to match the bridge protocol's per-tx limit (CCIP token-lane rate, CCTP V2         per-burn cap, etc.). Strategies on the peer chain treat the same value as         "max this adapter can deliver inbound per tx" to size their withdrawAll-style         requests. `0` = no enforcement at this layer (concrete adapters may still         apply hard protocol-level constants on top).

messageTransmitter().notice: Circle CCTP V2 Message Transmitter (message-only sends + inbound delivery).

minFinalityThreshold().notice: Minimum finality threshold sent on every transfer. Range: 1000–2000.         2000 = finalised (zero protocol fee, ~13 minute delay on Ethereum).         1000–1999 = fast finality (non-zero token-side fee, sub-minute delivery).         NOT initialised at declaration — that would only set the storage slot on         the implementation, not on the proxy. Governor must call         `setMinFinalityThreshold` post-deploy. Send-side guard catches the         pre-init mistake with a clear revert message.

minTransferAmount().notice: Lower bound on USDC transfers; governor-settable. Avoids dust burns that         waste gas + CCTP attestation latency on negligible amounts.

operator().notice: Account allowed to invoke `relay(message, attestation)` — the off-chain         attestation poller / relayer. Single address; governor-settable. CCTP is         pull-driven so this role is required at the adapter level; CCIP and         Superbridge don't need it.

quoteFee(address,uint256,bytes).notice: Quote the fee for the operation described by `(token, amount, payload)`.

relay(bytes,bytes).notice: Operator entry point: hand a Circle-signed CCTP message + attestation pair         to the local MessageTransmitter, then dispatch the payload to the destination         strategy.         CCTP V2 has two on-wire message shapes that both arrive here:         1. **Burn-message + hook** (sourced from `TokenMessenger.depositForBurnWithHook`).            The transport `sender` is the source-side `TokenMessenger`. The transport            `recipient` is the destination `TokenMessenger`, NOT this adapter. The            body is a CCTP burn body containing burnToken / mintRecipient / amount /            msgSender / feeExecuted / hookData. Auto-dispatch via            `handleReceiveMessage` on the mintRecipient is V2.1-only and not            universally available across Circle's chain deployments, so we DON'T rely            on it. Instead: we call `messageTransmitter.receiveMessage` (which credits            USDC to this adapter as the configured mintRecipient), then parse the burn            body ourselves and call `_deliver` with the authoritative `amount -            feeExecuted` and the hookData (our application envelope). This mirrors            the older `AbstractCCTPIntegrator.relay()` pattern, which has been            exercised in production.         2. **Pure message** (sourced from `MessageTransmitter.sendMessage`). Transport            `sender` and `recipient` are both this adapter (CREATE3 parity). The body            is our application envelope directly. `messageTransmitter.receiveMessage`            triggers our own `handleReceiveFinalizedMessage` hook, which calls            `_handleInbound` and dispatches.

sendMessage(bytes).notice: Send a message-only payload to the configured peer.

sendMessageAndTokens(address,uint256,byt.notice: Send a token transfer alongside a message to the configured peer.

setMaxTransferAmount(uint256).notice: Governor sets the per-tx token amount ceiling. Set to match the bridge         protocol's actual per-tx limit (CCIP lane rate, CCTP burn cap, etc.).         `0` disables the check (e.g., canonical bridges with no per-tx limit).

strategists(address).notice: Strategists list — actors permitted to flip the `paused` flag on a lane.         The governor also has these powers.

transferGovernance(address).notice: Transfers Governance of the contract to a new account (`newGovernor`). Can only be called by the current Governor. Must be claimed for this to complete

transferToken(address,uint256).notice: Sweep a stuck asset (or native via `_asset == address(0)`) to the governor.         Recovery only — used to rescue mistaken sends or drain stale refund balances.

userdoc.notice: Atomic bidirectional adapter over Circle CCTP V2.           - Outbound (`sendMessageAndTokens`): burn USDC via `depositForBurnWithHook` with             the wrapped envelope as the hook data. The recipient mint amount equals the             burn amount minus CCTP's protocol fee (0 for finalised threshold, > 0 for             fast finality). The fee is absorbed by the protocol; the receiving strategy             accounts on the actual landed amount.           - Outbound (`sendMessage`): post a hook-only message via the MessageTransmitter.           - Inbound (`handleReceiveFinalizedMessage`): CCTP MessageTransmitter has minted             USDC to this adapter. Validate source domain + sender against the lane config,             then forward the actual minted amount to the destination strategy.         CCTP has no native bridge fee — `_quoteFee` returns 0 and the AbstractAdapter's         `msg.value` plumbing simply refunds whatever the caller forwarded.
